name: coPutValue
title: coPutValue
tables: Deprecated Function Replaced by CANopen Basic Functions.
void coPutValue( char envVar[], long value, dword errCode[] );

The function sets the value of an environment variable of the type integer. Note In contrast to the putValue function of CANoe, with this function the name for runtime can be specified via a string and it does not already have to be known at translation time. This means, for example, it can be combined at runtime with the help of string functions.

Note In contrast to the putValue function of CANoe, with this function the name for runtime can be specified via a string and it does not already have to be known at translation time. This means, for example, it can be combined at runtime with the help of string functions.

Note In contrast to the putValue function of CANoe, with this function the name for runtime can be specified via a string and it does not already have to be known at translation time. This means, for example, it can be combined at runtime with the help of string functions.
envVar Name of the environment variable
value Value of the environment variable
errCode Error code buffer (is entered in index 0 of the field
Example dword errCode[1];coPutValue("envVarStart", 1, errCode);if (errCode[0] == 0) { write( "Value of the environment variable successfully set" );}
